message:
chore(beads): add Wills & Estate Planning launch-article epic (hgjp + 3 subtasks)

Creates bead `jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp` as the umbrella
epic for the upcoming `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`
long-form launch article, plus three scaffolding child beads.

hgjp (epic)
-----------
Covers the full launch: article route, hero illustration, 5+
interactive visualizations, OG/Twitter images, writing-index and
command-palette registration, and a tri-tab non-terminal install
section that embeds a copy-paste agent prompt wired to
jsm.md-gated short-lived install codes.

Target audience is deliberately non-technical (office workers,
parents, retirees). Secondary purpose: the "paste-a-prompt-into-
your-agent" install pattern generalizes to any paid skill on
jsm.md, so the article doubles as a template for future non-dev
skill launches.

Cross-repo scope noted: the jsm.md side (DB migration + endpoints
+ install-card UI) is tracked in the jeffreys-skills.md bead graph
and is a blocking dependency for the install section. Article can
ship with a "coming soon" placeholder if jsm.md lags.

Acceptance gates are already written into the bead body: bun
build/tsc/lint green, ubs green on changed files, Playwright smoke
covering article render + TOC nav + visualization mount + install-
tab switching.

hgjp.1 — Scaffold article route + metadata
hgjp.2 — Commission/generate hero illustration
hgjp.3 — Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)
All three wired to hgjp via parent-child dependency so
`br ready --label phase-1` picks them up in the right order.

No code change in this commit — beads only.

files.patch:
@@ -164,6 +164,13 @@ {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-g1f","title":"Feature: Copy-to-clipboard for code blocks and commands","description":"# Copy-to-Clipboard for Code Blocks ## Overview Add one-click copy functionality to all code snippets on the TL;DR page, especially the important curl installation command in the footer CTA. ## Current State - Footer CTA has `` block with curl command but no copy button - Users must manually select and copy - No visual feedback on copy success ## Locations to Add Copy Button ### 1. Footer CTA (Priority) ```tsx // app/tldr/page.tsx lines 103-106 curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/.../install.sh | bash -s -- --yes --mode vibe ``` ### 2. Tool Card Tech Stack (Optional) - Could add "Copy install command" if we add install commands per tool ## Component Design ### CopyButton Component ```tsx interface CopyButtonProps { text: string; className?: string; } function CopyButton({ text, className }: CopyButtonProps) { const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false); const handleCopy = async () => { await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text); setCopied(true); setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 2000); }; return ( ); } ``` ### CodeBlock Wrapper ```tsx function CodeBlock({ code, language }: { code: string; language?: string }) { return ( 
        {code}
); } ``` ## Visual Design - Button position: Top-right corner of code block - Hidden by default, shows on hover (desktop) - Always visible on mobile (no hover) - States: - Default: Copy icon (lucide `Copy`) - Success: Checkmark icon (lucide `Check`) + green tint - Error: X icon + red tint (rare, clipboard permission denied) ## Animation - Icon swap: Fade/scale transition between Copy and Check - Success state: Brief green glow on button - Toast notification (optional): "Copied to clipboard!" ## Technical Notes - Use `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` (modern API) - Fallback for older browsers: `document.execCommand('copy')` - Handle permission denied gracefully ## Files to Create/Modify - `components/copy-button.tsx` (new) - Reusable copy button - `components/code-block.tsx` (new) - Code block with copy - `app/tldr/page.tsx` - Use CodeBlock for footer CTA ## Accessibility - Clear aria-label describing action - Focus ring visible - Screen reader announces "Copied" on success - Works with keyboard (Enter/Space) ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Copy button appears on code blocks - [ ] Click copies text to clipboard - [ ] Visual feedback shows success (checkmark) - [ ] Feedback resets after 2 seconds - [ ] Works on mobile (button always visible) - [ ] Accessible via keyboard - [ ] Graceful error handling if clipboard unavailable","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-12T22:52:23.133944385Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T16:37:56.200248505Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T16:37:56.200181350Z","close_reason":"Footer CTA on /tldr page already has copy-to-clipboard functionality (app/tldr/page.tsx lines 82-147) with Copy/Check icons and visual feedback. Standalone CopyButton component also exists.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-g1f","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-0ft","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-g1f","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-r0i","type":"child","created_at":"2026-02-08T19:39:33Z","created_by":"import"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-gy1","title":"Design and build TldrToolGrid layout component","description":"# Task: Design and Build TldrToolGrid Layout Component ## Overview Create the grid layout component that arranges TldrToolCard components on the page. Must handle grouping by category and responsive layout. ## Component Requirements ### Props Interface ```typescript interface TldrToolGridProps { tools: TldrTool[]; category: 'primary' | 'supporting' | 'all'; className?: string; } ``` ### Layout Behavior #### Desktop (xl+) - Primary tools: 2-3 column grid, larger cards - Supporting tools: 3-4 column grid, compact cards #### Tablet (md-xl) - Primary tools: 2 column grid - Supporting tools: 2-3 column grid #### Mobile (` in a terminal, which excludes 90% of the target market. The article serves a secondary purpose: its a **case study in agent-friendly UX**. The paste-a-prompt-into-an-agent pattern generalizes to any paid skill on jsm.md, not just wills, so the article doubles as a template for future non-technical launches. ## Architectural pillars - **No public GitHub repo** for skill content. Everything gated behind jsm.md auth. - **Short-lived single-use install codes** minted server-side, bound to `(user_id, skill_id, exp, consumed_at)`. - **Agent-driven install**: the gated bundle URL is embedded in a copy-paste prompt that the users Claude/Codex agent executes. One tool-use approval on the desktop, no terminal keystrokes from the user. - **Works on every OS** (macOS / Windows / Linux) because the agent handles path/unzip — no native helper, no code signing required. - **Bundle watermark** inside the ZIPs README with user_id + timestamp. Deterrent against casual leaks; does not prevent determined piracy (acknowledged trade-off). ## Cross-repo scope - **This repo (`jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site`)**: the article + hero illustration + 5 custom visualizations + the agent-readable primer markdown in `/public/` + OG images + writing-index registration + command-palette entry + Playwright test. - **`/dp/jeffreys-skills.md`** (separate bead graph): DB migration + two endpoints + install-card UI + generic non-technical-user guide page. Tracked in that repos .beads/. ## Acceptance criteria - Article live at `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` with hero, TOC, 8+ long-form sections, 5 interactive visualizations, OG + Twitter images, and the tri-tab install section. - Primer markdown downloadable from the article. - Article visible in the writing index + command palette. - `bun run build` green, `bun tsc --noEmit` green, `bun lint` green, `ubs` green on changed files. - Playwright e2e smoke test covers: article renders, TOC links jump, visualizations mount, install-tab switches. - Depends on jsm.md beads being shipped for the install prompt URLs to resolve; acceptable to ship article first with a "coming soon" placeholder in the install section if jsm.md side is not ready.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:33:55.236585249Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","title":"Scaffold article route + metadata (app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx)","description":"Create `app/writing/wills-and-estate-planning/page.tsx` modeled on `app/writing/slack-mattermost-migration/page.tsx`: - `export const metadata` with title "Drafting a Serious Estate Plan on a Saturday | Jeffrey Emanuel" (or final chosen title), description, and `alternates.canonical` = `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning`. - `articleSchema` JSON-LD (@type Article) with headline, datePublished (2026-04-XX on launch), author, publisher, mainEntityOfPage, `about` array including: Estate Planning, Wills, Trusts, AI Agents, Agent Skills, Claude Code, Codex, Probate, Incapacity Planning. - Default export renders `` and ``. ## Why this bead exists Next.js App Router needs the route stub in place before the component file can be referenced. Keeping it tiny + isolated lets us ship the article component independently. ## Acceptance criteria - File exists with metadata + JsonLd + default export. - `bun tsc --noEmit` clean (will error until the article component is also scaffolded; that is OK — this bead blocks on the component scaffold bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.1","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:23.442042493Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","title":"Commission/generate hero illustration (assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp)","description":"Create the hero illustration for the article, paralleling `assets/slack_migration_post_illustration.webp`. ## Concept A warm, editorial scene — a kitchen table under soft lamplight with a stack of important documents (will, deed, 401(k) form, a crypto hardware wallet, a passport), a laptop open showing an agent chat window, and a small glowing parrot perched on a chair back. The parrot is the visual anchor: it references Jeffreys X post line about "your beloved parrot that has another 20 years of life remaining." Warm purple/amber/emerald glow, same palette as the slack illustration. ## Workflow 1. Generate candidates via the preferred pipeline (likely Midjourney/SDXL/etc. via Jeffreys existing image-gen tooling). 2. Pick one, upscale, convert to WebP at ~560px wide display size (with 2x source for retina). 3. Place at `assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp`. 4. Reference from the article via `import illustration from "@/assets/wills_estate_post_illustration.webp"`. ## Acceptance criteria - WebP file exists at the expected path. - Used as the hero image via Next `` pattern. - Alt text is descriptive: "Warmly-lit desk scene with estate-planning documents, a laptop running an AI agent, and a parrot — evoking an estate plan drafted at home with the help of an AI skill." - File size under ~700 KB (slack hero is 646 KB).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.2","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:36.015480119Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","title":"Build jargon glossary (lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts)","description":"Create `lib/wills-estate-jargon.ts` modeled exactly on `lib/slack-migration-jargon.ts`: export a `getJargon(term: string)` function that returns a `{ term, definition, ... }` record. ## Why The article uses the `` component to produce dotted-underline tooltips over technical terms. Estate planning has a dense jargon layer (QTIP, GRAT, SLAT, ILIT, QDOT, DSUE, GST, QPRT, CRT, CLT, DAF, POD, TOD, RLT, POA, ERISA, Medicaid lookback, portability, step-up basis, elective share, community property, pretermitted heir, ancillary probate, Ulysses clause, etc.), and the article explicitly targets non-technical readers. Tooltips let us say "ILIT" in the text without derailing the reader. ## Term list (starter — expand as article sections go in) - will, trust, revocable-trust, irrevocable-trust, pour-over-will, holographic-will - POA, durable-POA, springing-POA, healthcare-proxy, living-will, HIPAA, POLST-MOLST - beneficiary, contingent-beneficiary, per-stirpes, per-capita, portability, DSUE - probate, intestacy, elective-share, pretermitted-heir, ancillary-probate, homestead - QTIP, credit-shelter-trust, QDOT, ILIT, GRAT, SLAT, IDGT, QPRT, DAPT, dynasty-trust - step-up-basis, disclaimer, §6166, 706-portability, GST-exemption - titling, joint-tenancy, tenancy-by-entirety, community-property, TOD-deed, Lady-Bird-deed - ERISA, 401k-spousal-consent, IRA-stretch, Secure-Act - guardian, trustee, executor, fiduciary, co-trustee, successor-trustee - domicile, snowbird, multi-state-probate - asset-protection, Medicaid-lookback, SNT, ABLE, incentive-trust, Ulysses-clause - NFA-gun-trust, crypto-seed, self-custody, legacy-contact - charitable: DAF, CRT, CLT, private-foundation - OBBBA, $15M-exemption, basis-dominance ## Structure per term ```ts { term: "QTIP", full: "Qualified Terminable Interest Property trust", definition: "A trust for the surviving spouse where they get income for life, but the remainder goes to beneficiaries you chose (often first-marriage children). Prevents a widowed spouse from rewriting the plan and disinheriting the first marriage.", source: { label: "IRC §2056(b)(7)", href: "..." } // optional } ``` ## Acceptance criteria - File exists, ~40+ terms defined. - Imported and used via `` inline in the article component. - Each definition is plain-English (grandma-test: a reader who has never heard the term should understand it in one sentence).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.3","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:34:58.049538940Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","title":"Scaffold article component (components/wills-estate-article.tsx)","description":"Create `components/wills-estate-article.tsx` as a "use client" component modeled exactly on `components/slack-migration-article.tsx`. ## Scope of this bead (scaffold only — sections land in follow-up beads) - Imports: React, Next `Image`, Next fonts (Crimson_Pro, JetBrains_Mono, Bricolage_Grotesque), lucide-react icons (tentative: FileDown, ScrollText, ShieldCheck, HeartPulse, HandCoins, Landmark, Sparkles, BookOpen), `MathTooltip`, `getJargon` from `@/lib/wills-estate-jargon`, `getScrollMetrics` from `@/lib/utils`, and illustration asset. - Dynamic imports for the five visualizations (TierTriageViz, AxiomCoherenceViz, IntakePhasesViz, DeliverablesTreeViz, AntiPatternCardsViz) from `./wills-estate-visualizations`, each `{ ssr: false }` because they use browser APIs / framer-motion. - Font variables set up the same way as slack article (`--font-crimson`, etc). - Wrapper components defined locally (these can be copy-pasted from slack article with the `sm-` → `wep-` class-name rename if any styles diverge; otherwise keep `sm-` so we share the stylesheet): `J` (jargon tooltip), `EC` (editorial container, 800px centered), `Divider`, `Code`, `Mono`, `SectionHeader`, `Sub`, `RefTable`. - Primer download button constant: `PRIMER_HREF = "/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md"`, `PRIMER_FILENAME = "WILLS_AND_ESTATE_PLANNING_PRIMER.md"`. - Main exported component `WillsEstateArticle` with: - `scrollProgress` state + scroll listener (copy pattern from slack). - IntersectionObserver-driven section reveal (copy pattern from slack, reuses `sm-fade-section` / `sm-visible` classes in globals.css). - Outer `div` with ref, role="main", className combines `sm-scope sm-body` + font variable classes, same background `#020204` and text `#f8fafc`. - Scroll progress bar rendered at top. - Empty section placeholders (stubs with TODO comments) for each section bead to fill in: hero, download-cta, intro, catalog, install, who-this-is-for, core-insight, sample-session, produces, workflow, anti-patterns, verification, tldr. ## Why a single scaffold bead Every content-section bead will modify this file. Scaffolding it once means downstream section beads can land in parallel without file-creation conflicts. Per AGENTS.md "Backwards Compatibility & File Sprawl" rule: one article component, not ten. ## Acceptance criteria - File exists, `bun tsc --noEmit` is clean once the route + jargon beads land too. - Has placeholder stubs for every section. - Uses the existing `sm-*` CSS classes until we confirm we need a `wep-*` namespace (decided in the styles bead).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.4","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:20.549272285Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","title":"Register article in lib/content.ts + command palette","description":"Two small registrations so the article surfaces in the sites navigation: ## 1. `lib/content.ts` Add an entry to whichever array drives the writing listing (likely `writingPosts` / `writing` / similar; grep for where slack-mattermost-migration is registered). Include: - slug: `wills-and-estate-planning` - title: final article title - description: ~140 char hook suitable for the writing index card - date: publication date - tags: `["ai", "skills", "estate-planning", "wills", "life-planning"]` - href: `/writing/wills-and-estate-planning` - hero image reference ## 2. `components/command-palette.tsx` (Cmd+K search) Add to the navigation/search array so `Cmd+K` → "wills" finds the article. Pattern: same as how the slack-mattermost article is registered. ## Why this bead is separate These are tiny mechanical edits in existing registry files. Splitting from the article component bead avoids merge conflict with content sections and keeps the article discoverable the moment it goes live. ## Acceptance criteria - Article shows up on `/writing`. - `Cmd+K` → "wills" / "estate" / "planning" surfaces the article.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.5","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:31.551381415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} +{"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","title":"Write agent-readable primer markdown (public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md)","description":"Write `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md` — a single-file primer that a paying subscriber can download from the article and paste into any fresh Claude / Codex / ChatGPT session to boot the agent into the wills-estate-planning mindset. ## Purpose This is the offline-friendly equivalent of the skill itself. The full skill is 201 files, ~24k lines, ~1 MB — too big to paste into a context window. The primer is a lossy but coherent summary the agent can use even without the full skill installed. Acts as: 1. **Proof-of-value tease** for visitors who are not yet paying subscribers (they can see the shape of the skill). 2. **Emergency fallback** for a paying subscriber whose agent setup isnt cooperating — they can paste this and still get useful output. 3. **The thing the "Download the operator primer as Markdown" button on the article points at** (see `PRIMER_HREF` in the article scaffold bead). ## Structure (draws from SKILL.md at ~/jeffreys_skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill/) - `# Wills & Estate Planning — Operator Primer` - TL;DR: what estate planning really is (coordination, not drafting), the 11 axioms condensed to the 5 most important ones, and the 2026 OBBBA $15M exemption reality. - The kernel: axioms 0-11 in one-liner form with a sentence of why each matters. - Wealth-tier routing (Tier 1-5 with one sentence each). - The 9 intake phases as a numbered list. - The 9-step workflow as a numbered list. - The top 15 anti-patterns. - The verification-first discipline (live-law checks). - Output contract summary (intake/, analyses/, deliverables/). - A short disclaimer: this primer is educational; an attorney signs the documents. - Footer: "To get the full skill with all 17 subagents, 45 deliverable templates, and 135 reference files, subscribe at https://jeffreys-skills.md/ and install via the instructions at https://jeffreys-skills.md/skills/wills-and-estate-planning-skill" ## Format - Plain Markdown. - ~8-12 KB (smaller than slack primers 185 KB — this is deliberately a condensed version, not a complete operator guide). - Semantic headings so an agent can index it. ## Acceptance criteria - File exists at `public/wills-and-estate-planning-primer.md`. - Articles download button successfully serves it as `application/octet-stream` with the configured filename. - Content is self-contained: an agent with just this primer and no skill installed can still conduct a useful basic estate-planning intake.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp.6","depends_on_id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hgjp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-18T22:35:51.386730372Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]} {"id":"jeffrey_emanuel_personal_site-hio","title":"FEATURE: Source Code Research for All Flywheel Tools","description":"# FEATURE: Source Code Research for All Flywheel Tools ## Purpose Before writing content for the /tldr page, we need accurate, detailed information about each tool. This requires systematic source code review of all flywheel tools to extract: 1. **Accurate functionality description** - What does the code actually do? 2. **Implementation details** - What language? What frameworks? Performance characteristics? 3. **Integration points** - How does it actually connect to other tools in the ecosystem? 4. **Key features** - What are the standout capabilities? 5. **Usage patterns** - How is it typically invoked/used? ## Why Source Code Review is Critical - Marketing copy can be vague; code doesn't lie - Ensures technical accuracy - Discovers integrations that might not be documented - Provides specific implementation highlights (e.g., "60fps TUI via Bubble Tea") - Validates claimed features ## Tools to Research (12+ total) ### Primary Flywheel Tools: | Tool | Path | Language | |------|------|----------| | NTM | /data/projects/ntm | Go | | SLB | /data/projects/slb | Go | | MCP Agent Mail | /data/projects/mcp_agent_mail | TypeScript | | Beads Viewer | /data/projects/beads_viewer | Go | | UBS | /data/projects/ultimate_bug_scanner | Python | | CM (CASS Memory) | /data/projects/cass_memory_system | TypeScript | | CASS | /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search | Rust | ### Supporting Tools: | Tool | Path | Language | |------|------|----------| | DCG | /data/projects/destructive_command_guard | Go | | GIIL | /data/projects/giil | Go | | XF | /data/projects/xf | Go | | Source to Prompt TUI | /data/projects/source_to_prompt_tui | TypeScript/Bun | | Repo Updater | /data/projects/repo_updater | Bash | | Flywheel Setup | /data/projects/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup | Bash | ## Research Output Format Each tool research task should produce a structured summary: ``` Tool: [Name] GitHub: [URL] Language: [Primary language] Stars: [Count] ## What It Does [2-3 sentences - accurate based on code review] ## Why It's Useful [2-3 sentences - value proposition] ## Implementation Highlights - [Key tech/framework] - [Performance characteristic] - [Architectural pattern] ## Synergies - [Tool A]: [How they integrate - specific to code] - [Tool B]: [How they integrate] ``` ## Execution Strategy - Use subagents (Task tool with Explore agent) for each tool - Run multiple research tasks in parallel where possible -
